results.label: Standard — science podcast with guest
results.output_summary: INTRO (~45 seconds / ~85 words): Opens with guest quote about the brain not pretending to be rational. Establishes the episode topic. Show name 'Curious Minds' appears within 20 seconds. Delivery notes in parentheses. OUTRO (~30 seconds / ~80 words): Callbacks to dream research, CTAs in correct order (subscribe, review, show notes for book). Alternate intro provided with host-led setup. No repeated wording between intro and outro.

A1.note: INTRO and OUTRO labeled with approximate durations.

A2.note: Opens with the specific guest quote. Show name within first 20 seconds.

A3.note: Natural spoken cadence with contractions. Outro uses different language than intro.

results.label: Minimal — only required fields
results.output_summary: INTRO: Host-led setup since no guest quote available. Hooks with a relatable morning routine failure scenario. Show name 'The Daily Grind' identified early. Direct, practical tone. OUTRO: Brief practical callback, subscribe CTA only (no other CTAs specified). Matches no-nonsense register.

A2.note: Intro hook is specific (morning routine failure), not generic. Show name present.

A3.note: Natural spoken delivery. No wording repeated between intro and outro.

results.label: European context — European wine podcast
results.output_summary: INTRO: Opens with the Burgundy winemaker's quote about her grandmother. Music bed cue noted. Show name 'Terroir Talk' identified early. European wine context authentic — references Burgundy, natural wine movement in France and Italy. OUTRO: Callbacks to the tradition-versus-innovation debate. CTAs: subscribe + website for wine list. Alternate intro with host-narrated setup about the natural wine divide.

A1.note: Both scripts labeled with durations. Music bed cue included.

A2.note: Opens with the specific grandmother quote. Show name within first 30 seconds.

A3.note: Natural spoken cadence. European wine terminology authentic. No repeated wording.
